abstract: Distills a logical relational-database design methodology into rules an agent applies while designing or reviewing a schema. Covers the design sequence, one-subject tables, atomic single-valued fields, candidate/primary/foreign keys, relationships with deletion rules and participation, the four levels of data integrity, business rules and views, and the bad-design antipatterns to avoid — all expressed as logical structure that is independent of any specific RDBMS and normalized by construction.
